title: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee.
body_text: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee. Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown fences, no commentary. The JSON MUST validate against this Pydantic JSON Schema for SummarizedContent: { "description": "Class for a single class label summary and description.", "properties": { "summary": { "title": "Summary", "type": "string" }, "description": { "title": "Description", "type": "string" } }, "required": [ "summary", "description" ], "title": "SummarizedContent", "type": "object" } System instructions from Cognee: You are a top-tier summarization engine. Your task is to summarize text and make it versatile. Be brief and concise, but keep the important information and the subject. Use synonim words where possible in order to change the wording but keep the meaning. Input text: output/cognee_codex_full_v2_bundle/nodes.jsonl I’m reading `output/cognee_codex_full_v2_bundle/nodes.jsonl` to extract its contents and return the required JSON summary. I’ve confirmed the file is a large JSONL node bundle. Next I’m sampling its structure and node-type distribution so the summary reflects the dataset rather than a few early rows. {"summary":"A JSONL research graph bundle of AI and co

commands_run: jq -r '.metadata.source_path // "null"' output/cognee_codex_full_v2_bundle/nodes.jsonl | sed 's#/[^/]*$##' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | sed -n '1,30p'

turns.text: I’m reading `output/cognee_codex_full_v2_bundle/nodes.jsonl` to extract its contents and return the required JSON summary.
I’ve confirmed the file is a large JSONL node bundle. Next I’m sampling its structure and node-type distribution so the summary reflects the dataset rather than a few early rows.

{"summary":"A JSONL research graph bundle of AI and computer vision notes, mainly on world models, 3D/4D reconstruction, Gaussian Splatting, and related papers, feeds, repos, claims, and evidence spans.","description":"This file contains 1,331 structured Cognee nodes extracted from daily research materials in April 2026. It is dominated by Paper, EvidenceSpan, Claim, and PerformanceClaim entries, linking source snippets from feeds, digests, repositories, and paper summaries into a knowledge graph. The content centers on world models, video and multimodal generation, 3D/4D scene understanding, reconstruction pipelines, agent systems, and benchmark or performance statements, with both English and Korean text represented."}
